evidence_quotes.quote: These licenses have no end date, but can be taken away whenever the seller feels like it.
the product you bought has been removed.

paragraphs.text: Dear representatives, Thank you for looking at our feedback. Nowadays games are often sold digitally. Even if you try to buy a physical copy of a game that has increasingly often become a box with a download code in it or something similar. This results in consumers owning a license rather than a physical copy of a game. These licenses have no end date, but can be taken away whenever the seller feels like it. It is becoming more common that one day you wake up and find that the product you bought has been removed. This may be in the hopes that you buy the company's new product instead of continuing to use the product you had and were happy with. Other times this may be to cut costs. It's understandable that a company can't continue to provide servers forever, but when consumers are willing to host their own server or simply play offline then why should the product be taken from them? Please consider stopping companies from doing this. Thank you for your efforts.
